title: Silly Syllable Rap Song. Clap or Chop the Syllables to spell common English words.
description:
A syllable is a unit of pronunciation containing a vowel and possibly surrounding consonants. The number of times that you hear the sound of a vowel is the number of syllables in a word. Syllables can be used to break a larger word into smaller parts when reading or spelling English words. When spelling longer words, using syllables is a more efficient strategy than using sounds. This Phonics song has follow along actions. Karate Chop your wrist whenever to say a syllable. 

Download the PowerPoint - For Subscribers Only!

https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1jP5f6dE15O7yPwf00gO-Q3juujv50wnG/edit?usp=sharing&ouid=109193247342571554306&rtpof=true&sd=true

 Here are some of the main key guidelines for breaking a word in a syllables.

1. Make sure every syllable has a vowel. This is the number one and most important rule of dividing a word into syllables.

2. Always divide between the two words inside a compound word. 

3. Always divide between the base word and the suffix if the suffix contains a vowel. 

4. Keep units of sound together, don't divide a syllable in between a diagraph, trigraph, quadhraph, blend and diphthong.

5. Be mindful that breaking a word in the syllables is dependant on pronunciation and accents. 

6. Patterns of vowels and consonants can be used to break a word into syllables if you do not know what the word sounds like.

title: Bossy R Rap Song | Phonics and Spelling Rules for R Controlled Vowels | AR OR ER IR UR |
description:
Resource Downloads: https://mrspelling.com/
Support Mr Spelling: https://ko-fi.com/mrspelling

Bossy R changes the sound of the vowel that comes before it. The vowel doesn't say its usual sound, instead, the R takes control and creates a new sound, called an R-controlled vowel. Common Bossy R spelling patterns include: AR as in car, OR as in fork, IR as in bird, ER as in fern and UR as in burn.

Here are the spelling rules: Double R is not bossy. Bossy R does not work across syllables. The schwa can override Bossy R. W can change the sound of the Bossy R vowel. 

Learning how Bossy R works helps students improve their reading, spelling, and pronunciation, because they begin to recognize these special vowel patterns quickly and accurately. The modern rap phonics song teaches Bossy R in a fun and engaging way.

title: Add VES to English nouns ending in F or FE to make a plural. #phonics #pluralnouns  #spelling
Plural Rule 4: Add -VES to Make English Plurals for Nouns Ending In F and FE.  Spelling Rap Song
description:
Changing F or FE to VES is English plural rule number 4. To make these nouns plural, we replace the F or FE with VES, such as leaf becoming leaves, knife becoming knives, and wolf becoming wolves. However, not every word that ends in F follows this rule, because some simply add -s, such as roof becoming roofs or chef becoming chefs. A double FF is never dropped to add -VES. When we change F or FE to VES, the meaning changes from singular (one) to plural (more than one). This spelling rule applies to many nouns, but remember there are seven plural rules in total, so keep learning and practicing them all. The rule is taught through a catchy rap song with clear examples to help students remember it easily.

The 7 Plural Rules
1. Just add –S.
2. Add -ES to words ending in CH,SH,Z,S or X.
3. Change the Y to an I and add -ES.
4. Drop the F or FE, add -VES.
5. Base word ends in O, sometimes –S, sometimes -ES.
6. Use the same word. (defective nouns)
7. Use a different word. (irregular plurals, Latin plurals)

title: The Schwa Phonics Song /ə/. Sound Out the Most Common Vowel Sound in English. Educational Rap. 🎵
description:
The Schwa is the most common vowel sound in English and it doesn't even have its own letter. The Schwa makes a lazy 'uh' sound or sometimes an 'ih' sound. The Schwa can be found in vowels in unstressed syllables. Any vowel can be a schwa including the letter Y. Often vowels will reduce to Schwas as the speaker tries talk more quickly and fluently. 

This educational rap teaches students how to recognize and pronounce the schwa sound (ə) found in unstable syllables throughout the English language.

Plural Rule 3: -IES Plurals Rap Song. Change Y to I and Add ES to Make English Nouns Plural.
description:
Changing Y to I and adding ES is the third English plural rule and is used when nouns end in a consonant + Y. Do not use this rule if the word ends in a vowel + Y, such as boy or key. Do not apply this rule to proper nouns or uncountable nouns. When we change Y to IES, it changes the meaning from singular to plural. This plural rule applies to many nouns, but remember there are 7 plural rules in total, so keep learning them all. The spelling rule is taught via a catchy rap song with clear examples. 

The 7 Plural Rules
1. Just add –S.
2. Add -ES to words ending in CH,SH,Z,S or X.
3. Change the Y to an I and add -ES.
4. Drop the F or FE, add -VES.
5. Base word ends in O, sometimes –S, sometimes -ES.
6. Use the same word. (defective nouns)
7. Use a different word. (irregular plurals, Latin plurals)

Lyrics:

If yes, change the Y to an I and add ES.

The Letter Y Has Four Main Sounds. Phonics Rap Song
description:
The English Letter Y has four main sounds explained in this rap song. The sound of the letter Y will change depending on its position in a word. This is because Y often replace I at the end of a word as English words rarely end in I. 

The Sounds:
At the start of a syllable or a word, the Letter Y makes the consonant sound like in the words Yellow, yarn and kayak. /j/

In the middle of syllable, the letter Y can make the short vowel sound /ĭ/ like in the words gym, myth and symbol.

At the end of a word, the letter Y can make the long vowel sound /ī/ like in the words my, fly and cry. Also in Y_E words, words ending in -FY and -PLY and unstressed open syllables. 

At the end of a word, the letter Y can make the long vowel sound /ē/ like in the words happy, baby and candy.

title: Apostrophe of Possession Rap Song. Grammar and Spelling Rules For Possessive Nouns.
description:
The apostrophe of possession is a piece of punctuation that shows that an object belongs to someone or something. It turns a noun into a possessive noun. This educational rap song will teach English learners when and how to use the apostrophe of possession. 

Rule 1
Add ’s to a singular noun to make a possessive noun.
Rule 2
If a noun is plural and ends in S, add only an apostrophe after the S in the word.
Rule 3
For irregular plurals that don’t end in S, use ’s to show ownership.

Do not use the apostrophe of possession on pronouns as pronouns have a possessive form.

title: English words don't end with the letter I. Spelling tips. #phonics #englishgrammar #spelling
IUVJ: English Words Don't End In I, U, V,  & J. The Never Ending Spelling Rule In a Rap Song.
description:
Traditional spelling rules inherited from Latin prevent native English words from ending in the letters I, U, V & J. If you hear the sound made by one of these letters at the end of the word, very often an alternative spelling pattern is used. Silent final E can be added to I, U and V to prevent them from being at the end of a word. A soft G spelling of DGE or GE is used instead of ending a word with the letter J. Foreign words or words borrowed from other cultures are often an exception to this rule.

I, U, Q, V & J, I have heard: These letters rarely appear at the end of a word.

Native English words rarely end with the letter I.
Spell with IE or IGH or use the letter y.
Native English words rarely end with the letter U.
Spell with UE or OO or use EW.
Native English word rarely end with the letter Q.
We know from our spelling rules that Q is followed by U.
Native English words rarely end with the letter V.
Instead those words are usually spelt with a  silent final E.
Native English words rarely end with the letter J.
Use DGE or GE, to spell in a soft G way.

title: Long Vowel A. English Spelling Patterns Rap Song. Eight Ways to Make the Sound A. Phonics /eɪ/.
description:
The long vowel A sound, represented phonetically as /ā/ or eɪ/, can be spelt many different ways. This phonics rap song provides and overview of the eight most common ways the long A sound can be spelt. The single letter A makes its long sound at the end of a syllable, as in the word baby. The digraph AI, found in rain, typically appears in the middle of words, while AY as in pay, is most often found at the end of words. The split digraph A_E, as seen in the word cake, is one of the most frequent ways to represent the long A sound, where the final silent E causes the A to say its name.

Ways to represent and spell the long A sound:

a as in baby
ai as in rain
ay as in pay
a_e as in cake
ea as in steak
ae as in sundae
ei as in reindeer
eigh as in weight
et as in ballet
ey as in survey
aigh as in straight
e_e as in suede

title: Spelling Rules: Words Don't End in the letter J #backtoschool #phonics #spelling #teachers
DGE or GE? The Final 'j' Sound d͡ʒ. Phonics and Spelling Rule Rap Song for Grade 2 or ESL
description:
When spelling English words they never end in the letter J. Instead they use a soft G spelling of either DGE or GE at the end of a word. Here are the spelling rules for the sound /d͡ʒ./

In a one syllable word, after a short vowel use DGE for the final 'j' sound.
In a one syllable word, after a long vowel use GE for the final 'j' sound.
In a one syllable word, after a vowel, then a consonant use GE for the final 'j' sound.
This spelling rule does not apply to multisyllabic words. The stress of a multisyllabic word can often affect the pronunciation of the vowel before the final J sound. EG cabbage sounds like 'cab- IJ.'

The video has been made for all audiences and can be used by adults studying English as a second language. 

Download the PowerPoint for free: 

https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1Bl5C4wt82ULF_6NxZicUG4elTqNaKgpc/edit?usp=sharing&ouid=109193247342571554306&rtpof=true&sd=true

Lyrics:

Native English words, don't end with the letter J.
So if a 'j' is heard, at the end of a word, it’s spelt in a soft G way.
In a one syllable word, after a short vowel use DGE./
The D protects the short vowel/ from the final silent E.
In a one syllable word, after a long vowel use GE./Final silent E, can change the vowel sound jumping G.

description:

AIR will always say ‘air’. This is the most common way of making the ‘air’ sound. It is mainly used in words that are nouns.

ARE will mostly likely say ‘air.’ This spelling is mainly used to spell words that are verbs. When it is by itself, it is the word are and is pronounced ‘ar’. 

EAR will most likely say ‘ear’ as in spear. It can also say ‘ur’ as in Earth. There are a few words in which it says ‘air’ such as pear, wear, swear, bear and tear.

title: Phonics: QU | Spell and Sound Out QU Words in this Phonics Rap Song. Digraph QU says 'kw'
description:
The digraph QU makes the sound 'kw' as in queen.

description:
The digraph TH can make three sounds:
/θ/ Th as in thorn is voiceless, it is made only with air, not your vocal cords.
/ð/ Th as in feather is voiced. It is made with vibration of the vocal cords. This is usually used in function words eg this, that, that. A silent E after a TH usually means the TH is voiced.

title: Long and Short Vowels | Spelling Rules | Learn How a Vowel Changes Its Sound | A Rap Song
description:
English Vowels can make a short sound (ă ĕ ĭ ŏ ŭ) or a long sound (ā ē ī ō ū).
Here are the spelling rules for when a vowel changes from a short sound to a long sound.
1. Silent Final E can jump one consonant to make a vowel say its long sound.
2. If two vowels are together they may work as a vowel team to possibly make the vowel say its long sound. 
3. If a vowel is at the end of a syllable it will make its long sound. This is called an open syllable
4. If I or O are followed by two consonants they will make their long sound.

Vowels can also make the schwa sound which is the unstressed centralised vowel sound. Vowels can also change sound because of the speakers accent. 

Lyrics

Vowels can be short ă ĕ ĭ ŏ ŭ
Vowels can be long. ā ē ī ō ū
Learn how a vowel can change its sound in this song.

title: Long Vowel O Spelling Patterns and Spelling Rules | O, OA, OW, O_E & OE | A Phonics Rap Song. /oʊ/
description:
This Rap song will teach listeners there are many different ways to spell the long vowel O sound.  /oʊ/

The letter O can make the long O sound at the end of a syllable or when followed by two consonants
The digraph OA can make the O sound and is usually found at the start or middle of a word. 
The digraph OW usually appears at the end of a word unless followed by L,N or R. This digraph can also make the sound ‘ow’ as in cow.
The split digraph O_E is the most common way to make the long ‘o’ sound. The silent E jumps one consonant to make the O say its long sound.
This rare digraph occurs at the end of one syllable words such as toe, foe, doe, Joe, woe and hoe. 
This rare quadgraph occurs in the words dough, although and thorough. OUGH can make other sounds including ‘ow’ as in plough and ‘or’ as in bought.

title: Silent Final E | All Purposes and Spelling Rules in an Advanced Educational Rap Song.
description:
The Silent Final E is used in spelling for a wide range of purposes. This educational rap song covers all seven jobs of Silent Final E.

1. It jumps a single consonant to make a short vowel long.
2. Words in English, don’t end with ‘u’ or ‘v.’/So fix those words by using silent final ‘e.’
3. It changes sound of ‘c’ and ‘g.’ It makes ‘c’ say ‘s.’ It makes ‘g’ say ‘j.’
4. Every syllable in a word needs a vowel, so use silent final ‘e’ when no vowel is found.
5. It makes singular words not look like a plural.
6. It clarifies meaning for words that sound the same.
7. If a silent ‘e’ after ‘th’ is found, /then change ‘th’ from a voiceless to voiced sound. 
8. Sometimes a silent final ‘e’ is added to a word/for no reason at all, English is absurd!

title: The Doubling Rule. An Advanced Spelling Rule Rap Song For Doubling The Last Letter.
description:
The doubling spelling rule, also known as the "1-1-1 rule," helps determine when to double the final consonant of a word before adding a suffix. The suffix added must start with a vowel or be the suffix -y. Here’s a summary of the rule:

If a one syllable word ends with short vowel followed by consonant, double the final letter before adding a suffix. 

Only apply this rule to words with two or more syllables of the final syllable is stressed.

Words ending in w, x, or y do not follow this rule because these consonants are never doubled.

Then double the last letter and add your base word and suffix together. (Don’t double ‘x,’ ‘y,’ or ‘w.’)

title: THE SUFFIX -LY | All Spelling Rules In A Rap Song For Adding The Suffix -ly
description:
Spelling rules for adding the suffix -ly
1. Just add -ly
2. If a word ends with the letter ‘y’ Change the ‘y’ to an ‘i’ and add ‘ly’
3. If a word ends with the letters ‘ic’ Don’t add ‘ly’ instead add ‘ally’
4. If a word ends with the letters ‘le’ Change the ‘le’ to ‘ly’
5. If a word ends with the letter ‘e’ Don’t drop the ‘e’ just add ‘ly’

The suffix -ly can turn adjectives into adverbs and nouns into adjectives.

Lyrics

Suffix ‘ly’ Give it a try
It makes adjectives into adverbs Alright!
The quick runner ran He ran quickly
The slow car stopped It stopped slowly
Suffix ‘ly’ Give it a try
It makes nouns into adjectives Meaning like
The manly man ran With all his might
The girly girl twirled She twirled all night

Rule 1 It’s easy Just add ‘ly’
quick quickly  fastly is not a word  
slow slowly
friend friendly

title: THE SUFFIX -ING | All Spelling Rules For Adding The Suffix -ing In A Rap Song.
description:
Spelling Rules for adding the suffix -ing:
Rule 1 It’s easy add ‘ing’
Rule 2 If a one syllable word ends CVC (consonant vowel consonant)
Double the last letter, and add ‘ing’ (don’t double x, w or y) 
Rule 3 If it ends in a silent ‘e’ drop the ‘e’ add ‘ing’
Rule 4 If it ends in ‘c’ add ‘k’ then ‘ing’
Rule 5 If the final syllable of a (multisyllabic) word is stressed
Then double the last letter and add the rest
Rule 6 If the final syllable of a (multisyllabic) word isn’t stressed
Don’t double the last letter add ‘ing’ instead
Rule 7 If a word ends with the letters ‘ie’ 
Change the ‘ie’ to a ‘y’ and add ‘ing’

* British spellers will double the final ‘l’ of a multisyllabic word even when not stressed *
* Spelling rules will always have exceptions *

title: THE SUFFIX -ED |  All Spelling Rules In a  Rap Song For Adding The Suffix -ed To Verbs
description:
Here are all the spelling rules for adding the suffix ‘ed’ to words. The suffix ‘ed’ will turn a verb into a past tense verb and a noun into and adjective.

Rule 4: If it ends with a consonant, followed by a y (Rule 4) Change the ‘y’ into an ‘i’ and add ‘ed’

Rule 6: Sometimes the rules do not make sense, as some words have irregular past tense
Rule 7 British spellers will double the final ‘l’ of a  multisyllabic word even when not stressed
Rule 8  If a word ends in ‘c’ add ‘k’ before adding ‘ed’  For example: picnic becomes picnicked

title: Spelling Rule Rap: Adding -ves to make plurals from words that in 'f' or 'fe'
description:
If a base word ends in 'f' or 'fe,' drop the 'f' or 'fe' and add 'ves' to make in plural. Don't drop a double 'f.' This spelling rule has many exceptions. 


A spelling rule song by Mr Spelling

title: Spelling Rule Lesson: Making Plurals By Adding the Suffix -es
description:
If a base word ends in 'ch sh z x or s' add -es to make it plural. Exceptions include irregular plurals, Latin plurals and words where the 'ch' makes a /k/ sound. 
Download the lesson PowerPoint from:
https://drive.google.com/file/d/1cbdW4HUb-v7SkcGbvbfkHf4p4R1bc-BG/view?usp=sharing

title: Spelling Rule Lesson: Adding the Suffixes -ful and -fully
description:
For most words you can add the suffix -ful.
If the base word ends in a vowel + y, then just add the suffix –ful.
If the base word ends in a consonant + y, then change the 'y' to an 'i' and then add the suffix. 

Download the PowerPoint from:
https://drive.google.com/file/d/1282Clr9groBNbPxOs5fjSrbnzMWdlkrK/view?usp=sharing

title: Adverb Spelling Rule Lesson: Drop The 'y' Add 'ily' #spellingrules #adverbs
description:
To turn an adjective that ends in the letter 'y' into an adverb, change the 'y' into an 'i' and add the suffix 'ly'
For Example:
Happy = Happily
Angry = Angrily
